BREAKING: Fortune is reporting that Republicans are considering devastating cuts to Medicaid and Medicare. Fight back now >>
---

_**Fortune: "The GOP May Attempt Major Health Care or Welfare Cuts"**_
[**Sign on: tell Republicans not to slash safety net programs >>**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d739/2b07114c/a5efe3c0/731a05b2/3678568362/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/tell-the-gop-no-cuts?source=PT-20180107-Signature_DS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")
Team,

Donald Trump, Mitch McConnell and Paul Ryan met Friday to plan their next big legislative push -- and one of the top agenda items? Massive cuts to Medicare and Medicaid.

We can't allow Republicans to attack the programs that seniors and working families rely on most -- and we have to shut this down before they decide to move forward with their reckless proposal.

[**Senate Democrats need you standing with them right now. Sign your name to tell Republicans you will fight back against any cuts to Medicare and Medicaid**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d739/2b07114c/a5efe3c0/731a05b2/3678568362/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/tell-the-gop-no-cuts?source=PT-20180107-Signature_DS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")

First, Republicans raised taxes on working Americans with their disastrous tax scam. Now, they're shamelessly going after Medicare and Medicaid to pay for it -- and seniors and working families will be the ones caught in the crosshairs.

Senate Democrats have already made clear they'll fight tooth and nail to defend Medicare and Medicaid. But now they need you to show the GOP you have their backs.

**Add your name right now to show Republicans you 're ready to stand up for seniors and working families and prepared to fight all the GOP's attempts to gut Medicaid and Medicare:**

[**http://www.dscc.org/tell-the-gop-you-will-fight-back**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d739/2b07114c/a5efe3c0/731a05b2/3678568362/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/tell-the-gop-no-cuts?source=PT-20180107-Signature_DS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")

Thank you,

Team DSCC

**A quick note on donations, team:**

When we say that we're counting on grassroots donors to protect Senate Democrats, we're not kidding. In fact, a full 99% of the DSCC's contributions come from grassroots donors giving less than $200. We don't have the Koch brothers in our corner -- we have you. And every gift you give the DSCC is a gift we use to propel our Senate candidates to victory.

Together, we're going to take back the Senate, but we'll need the help of every single grassroots supporter to do it. name: Mitch Stewart, Democrats.org
subject: The Iowa caucus
body:
The only way Democrats will win this year is if lots of people get involved and get organized.
---
**Your contribution will help Democrats compete, organize, and win in all 50 states this year. Chip in $3 now:
**[DONATE](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523ca9/4067b0a/499bf05fd/7e5e061d/3595412944/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/10-years?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=em_20180108_ms_s5&source=em_20180108_ms_s5&refcode=em_20180108_ms_s5")**
**
Derek --

Ten years ago, I was working in Iowa for a guy named Barack Obama who had a pretty novel idea about how you win elections: neighbor by neighbor, block by block.

I learned just how true it is that one voice can change a room, that one room can change a city, and that one city can change the world. Creating change has always started at the local level -- and that's why it's been so encouraging to see the Democratic Party launch their Every ZIP Code Counts strategy.

When Democrats compete and organize everywhere, we win.

That's how we took back Congress from the GOP in 2006, that's how we elected Barack Obama as our President in 2008, and that's how we got Doug Jones elected in one of the reddest states in the country this past year. If we keep it up, it's also how Democrats will take back control of Congress in November.

**There's no doubt in my mind that the DNC is on the right track -- the only question is whether or not enough people will step up to help Democrats win. Contribute $3 today to support the DNC's organizing work as we kick off 2018.**

[QUICK DONATE: $3](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523ca9/4067b0a/499bf05fd/7e5e0624/3595412944/VEsO/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/10-years?amount=3&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=8+-+QUICK+DONATE+3&utm_campaign=em_20180108_ms_s5&source=em_20180108_ms_s5&refcode=em_20180108_ms_s5")
[QUICK DONATE: $10](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523ca9/4067b0a/499bf05fd/7e5e0625/3595412944/VEsP/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/10-years?amount=10&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=9+-+QUICK+DONATE+10&utm_campaign=em_20180108_ms_s5&source=em_20180108_ms_s5&refcode=em_20180108_ms_s5")
[QUICK DONATE: $25](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523ca9/4067b0a/499bf05fd/7e5e0622/3595412944/VEsHBQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/10-years?amount=25&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=10+-+QUICK+DONATE+25&utm_campaign=em_20180108_ms_s5&source=em_20180108_ms_s5&refcode=em_20180108_ms_s5")
[QUICK DONATE: $50](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523ca9/4067b0a/499bf05fd/7e5e0623/3595412944/VEsHBA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/10-years?amount=50&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=11+-+QUICK+DONATE+50&utm_campaign=em_20180108_ms_s5&source=em_20180108_ms_s5&refcode=em_20180108_ms_s5")
[QUICK DONATE: $100](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523ca9/4067b0a/499bf05fd/7e5e0620/3595412944/VEsHBw/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/10-years?amount=100&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=12+-+QUICK+DONATE+100&utm_campaign=em_20180108_ms_s5&source=em_20180108_ms_s5&refcode=em_20180108_ms_s5")
[Or donate another amount.](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523ca9/4067b0a/499bf05fd/7e5e0621/3595412944/VEsHBg/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/10-years?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=13+-+Or+donate+another+amount&utm_campaign=em_20180108_ms_s5&source=em_20180108_ms_s5&refcode=em_20180108_ms_s5")
Back in January 2008, when President Obama won an upset victory in the Iowa caucus, it happened for two reasons:

1. There was a powerful movement of people who believed that we could bring about positive change in our country -- but only if we worked together.

2. We got more people involved in the political process. First-time caucus goers, young people, and independents turned out in large numbers and it made all the difference.

**Here's the deal, Derek: What happened back then is actually not that different from what's happening today. People are ready for change and they are prepared to be the ones who create that change.**

That work starts now -- and with your help, we can take our fight to all 50 states.

[**Make a $3 contribution today to support the DNC's 2018 organizing work. We're counting on your support to elect Democrats nationwide this November.**](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523ca9/4067b0a/499bf05fd/7e5e062e/3595412944/VEsHAQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/10-years?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=14+-+Make+a++if+hpcraw+10+else+3+endif++contr&utm_campaign=em_20180108_ms_s5&source=em_20180108_ms_s5&refcode=em_20180108_ms_s5")

I'm excited to see what this year brings. Thanks for being a part of it.

Mitch

Mitch Stewart
Former Iowa State Caucus Director
Obama for America

Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, 430 South Capitol Street SE, Washington DC 20003 and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. Contributions or gifts to the Democratic National Committee are not tax deductible.

If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you, [update your contact information](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523ca9/4067b0a/499bf05fd/7e5e062f/3595412944/VEsHAA/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/user/manage?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=15+-+update+your+contact+information&utm_campaign=em_20180108_ms_s5&source=em_20180108_ms_s5&refcode=em_20180108_ms_s5"). Our email list is the best way we have of staying in regular contact with supporters like you across the country and letting you know about the work Democrats are doing. If you like staying in touch, but want to receive only the most important messages, click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523ca9/4067b0a/499bf05fd/7e5e062c/3595412944/VEsHAw/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/less-email?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=16+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180108_ms_s5&source=em_20180108_ms_s5&refcode=em_20180108_ms_s5"). Click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523ca9/4067b0a/499bf05fd/7e5e062d/3595412944/VEsHAg/ "http://www.democrats.org/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=17+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180108_ms_s5&source=em_20180108_ms_s5&refcode=em_20180108_ms_s5") to unsubscribe from our supporter list, but if you leave, it will be harder for you to stay involved in the organization that you've been such a critical part of. This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/40523ca9/4067b0a/499bf05fd/7e5e062a/3595412944/VEsHDQ/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/contact-the-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=18+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180108_ms_s5&source=em_20180108_ms_s5&refcode=em_20180108_ms_s5"), or just reply to this email! Thanks for supporting our party.

name: The White House
subject: Rebuilding rural America
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=999222e55f130660c1edae720187c0f8b8955c07878f5ac2daac6bd2e2bbf53036ec9cfdaf8cdfb6922e21f72056f7c18cdd351cd730518d

Rebuilding rural America

Today, President Donald J. Trump travels to Nashville, Tennessee, to deliver a major address at the American Farm Bureau Federation’s Annual Meeting. It will mark the first time since 1992 an American president has spoken at the event.

That symbolism is important. From his earliest days on the campaign trail, President Trump has highlighted the difference in prosperity between rural America and the country’s wealthiest coastal enclaves.

One sentence explains why this message resonated across America’s interior. It comes from the opening lines of a report from President Trump’s Agriculture and Rural Prosperity Task Force, which the President will share during his speech today: “While other sectors of the American economy have largely recovered from the Great Recession, rural America has lagged in almost every indicator.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=999222e55f1306606756648e3517b8c227e12e7c630484bce53a239951fd6f9b977446af19845db7590e3d2a3ecf737b6385560ca4755ad0
Watch the President’s speech live at 4:10 p.m. ET.

Bonus read: President Trump stands with local communities
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=999222e55f1306601f1c354cd23df5878df55bd0d148f31c6b9bc482c55a018a96785aabb1bdf9d9c9a51d2c50c419af54bacaacd8e951f2
against government overreach on land management.

Council of Economic Advisers: 2017 was a ‘banner year’

This week, the President’s Council of Economic Advisers weighed in on why “2017 was a banner year for the United States labor market.”

In the Council’s own words:

- “The economy added 2.1 million jobs and reversed job losses in critical sectors, most notably in manufacturing.”
- “Unemployment rates for America’s veterans, African-Americans, and Hispanics . . . reached historic lows in 2017.”
- “The first wave of tax reform job and wage gains should produce continued tightness in the labor market and thus bigger take-home checks for workers.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=999222e55f130660695e5dc0dec40fcd3f1461c0c3334f491821dd56614c47b20dd6cc7ea799170e69f0c22f5f822d8c381bdb6a6d105778
Read the CEA’s blog post about what happened to the American economy in 2017.

Behind the scenes at Camp David

President Trump spent the weekend at Camp David, hosting bicameral Congressional Leadership for a small-group discussion of 2018 legislative priorities that will build on the success of tax reform. The President specifically mentioned national security, infrastructure, the federal budget, and opioids as major topics of conversation.

“We have a lot of things to work on, a lot of things to accomplish,” the President said before boarding Marine One on Friday.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=999222e55f13066079459eaeee6e50c98d5c0213cf46394bde6ee073d03b8549b3a0a3b0f3ec578748005da2b1aa4d433c469eef51827fd8
Behind the scenes: See photos and remarks from the President’s weekend with Congressional leaders at Camp David.

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump listens to U.S. Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell, during a dinner with legislative leadership at Camp David | January 5, 2018 (Official White House Photo by Joyce N. Boghosian)

POTUS TODAY

After President Trump delivers remarks at the American Farm Bureau Federation’s Annual Convention in Nashville, Tennessee, he will attend the NCAA National Championship football game in Atlanta.

COMING UP

On Wednesday morning, President Trump will hold a meeting with his Cabinet.

Later, the President will welcome Prime Minister Erna Solberg of Norway to the White House. The two will discuss the bilateral ties between the United States and Norway, and how to jointly advance regional and global security, and economic prosperity.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=999222e55f1306606cf5e7937139734707e8f7f228fe2046d49c79305b7cc1ccc71c7f7e3fe2a66432241422b4b9c6fe8cd13399501f50d0
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=999222e55f130660fecc9c73a4e43124b72ba8f050e1c47ee04f5ad06809147a21813b585f83f6ebc2e88e5f4444a2b6f090ebbad82e2201
Contact the White House |

Unsubscribe

name: Donna Edwards
subject: A challenge for all of us
body:
[](http://action.donnaforprincegeorges.org/page/m/74feff6d/767134fb/5cf1bbef/43381099/2697731402/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dfe-email?refcode=em180108-nd")

Friends,
Wednesday night at midnight we will face the biggest deadline of this campaign. Our fundraising and our lists of supporters will be public.
You and I both know that everyone watching is waiting for a bump in the road -- a single sign that says our message of taking on the special interests and spreading success to every family can't work. **Let 's prove them wrong.**
**[Make a contribution here of $6 or more before our deadline at midnight Wednesday to show everyone watching that you are in this fight and ready to create a county that works for everyone -- not just those with money and power.](http://action.donnaforprincegeorges.org/page/m/74feff6d/767134fb/5cf1bbef/43381099/2697731402/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dfe-email?refcode=em180108-nd")**
Our community has been directed for years by big companies and special interests who convinced government officials to turn over as much land as possible to whoever could build anything the cheapest and quickest.
This short-term thinking has resulted in bad outcomes for workers and our county. We can and must do better. We are going create a government in Prince George's County that is open, transparent, accessible, and where the world competes to do business with our communities and our residents -- paying living wages, supporting small businesses and hiring local workers.
**Now taking on these entrenched interests will not be easy. In fact, they will likely spend large sums of money to stop us. But I have always believed that a strong grassroots campaign can take on anything and win. Do you?**
**[Show everyone where you stand and help us build this campaign with a contribution of $6 or more before our deadline Wednesday. I'm counting on you to be in this with me.](http://action.donnaforprincegeorges.org/page/m/74feff6d/767134fb/5cf1bbef/43381099/2697731402/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dfe-email?refcode=em180108-nd")**
Can't do this without you,
Donna

name: Debbie Wasserman Schultz
subject: Protect Medicare, Medicaid, and Social Security in 2018
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail
body:
| |
---
| |
---
| "You also have to bring spending under control. And not discretionary spending. That isn't the driver of our debt. The driver of our debt is the structure of Social Security and Medicare for future beneficiaries"
\-- _Florida Senator Marco Rubio_ ; November 2017 **"Next year [2018], we're going to have to get back to entitlement reform."**
\-- _House Speaker Paul Ryan_ ; December 2017 Republicans on both sides of the Capitol building have made themselves clear: **They want to target Medicare and Social Security in 2018.** We have to make our intentions for 2018 equally clear: **We will fight to protect Medicare, Medicaid, and Social Security for as long as it takes.** [_**Let the GOP know that when it comes to Medicare, Medicaid, and Social Security, we won't back down. Add your name to mine >>**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113fa7/622b7b5b/18878736/307bbb04/2082550126/VEsH/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e180106") | [](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113fa7/622b7b5b/18878736/307bbb04/2082550126/VEsE/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e180106")
---
President Trump and his allies in Congress are using a step-by-step deception to fool the American public.
First, they forced through their "tax reform" plan. This law is horrendous for many reasons, but the one I want to focus on is this: **It raises the deficit by almost $1.5 TRILLION in the next decade.**
That is, obviously, not sustainable. So the next step for Republicans is a crusade to "bring down the deficit" -- _**ignoring the fact that they're the ones who just increased it with their tax scam law.**_
Then finally, their next step: taking action to bring down the deficit. Will they take back the billionaire and corporate tax breaks to stop the deficit explosion? Of course not. As Speaker Ryan put it, they will "have to get back to entitlement reform" -- or as it's known in plain English: _cutting Medicare, Medicaid, and/or Social Security._
Now, Republicans don't want you to know all this. They're hoping that their deception will trick American families into thinking cutting back on Medicare, Medicaid, or Social Security is the rational, logical step to take. **But we know the truth.**
I, for one, will not allow Republicans to attack the financial stability of seniors, disabled individuals, or the families who take care of them -- not now, not ever. I see through their smoke screen and I will not back down.
[_**If you're with me, and are ready to commit to protecting Medicare, Medicaid, and Social Security in 2018, add your name to mine right now.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113fa7/622b7b5b/18878736/307bbb04/2082550126/VEsF/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e180106")
Thank you.
Debbie

name: Nina Turner, Our Revolution
subject: The Erica Snipes Garner Fellowship
body:
#
Friends,
Today, Our Revolution is joining thousands of people in celebrating the life of Erica Garner. Inspiring and fighting for her community, she spoke out against police violence and called millions to action in the fight for the economic liberation of African Americans. Every day she worked to improve the lives of her family and her community.

Erica Garner became a modern-day freedom fighter and activist after witnessing something no one should ever have to see--the murder of her father, Eric Garner. "I can't breathe" echoed through our country as millions watched Eric Garner repeat these words to the police officers who pushed him to the ground and choked him until his last breath.

Erica took her heartbreak and used it to fight back against police brutality in the hopes of creating a society where no one would ever have to experience the pain she suffered. I had the privilege of knowing Erica through the Bernie Sanders campaign. We shared a deep adoration for each other. Since meeting her, I was inspired greatly by her resilience and passion for justice. Erica spoke her truth about the injustices facing African Americans even when facing unfair criticism. In an interview I did with Erica last October, I was in awe of her compassion, poise, and strength.

Erica Garner's untimely passing has made my heart both heavy and reflective. Heavy because Erica was just beginning to hit her stride and make a mark in this world by challenging us to see the wicked truth about the impact of injustice. She called out the powerful and protested with the powerless, pushing us all out of our zones of comfort. My heart is reflective knowing the work on justice that Erica began lives on. We must take this pain we feel and turn it into action.

In an effort to honor her memory, Our Revolution is starting the Erica Snipes Garner Fellowship. This fellowship will research and advocate for criminal justice reform, improving police/community relations within the African American community, and ending health care disparities.

[**Add your name to be the first to get details about our Erica Snipes Garner Fellowship.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ad9e/bed43eb/37cfbd17/75fe9b51/3127774859/VEsH/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/the-erica-garner-snipes-fellowship")

Erica will always be a part of the Our Revolution family and we will work hard to continue justice advocacy work in her memory. While nothing can take away the pain and sadness of her loss, we will move forward knowing that Erica's legacy will lead to social, racial and economic justice for African Americans for years to come.

Finally, I'd like to leave you with this advice from Erica herself about what it takes to follow your dreams and be courageous:

_" Regroup. Organize. Strategize. Don't be a follower. Speak what you feel in your heart. Follow your instinct and if other people are not following you, then be that one to stand up by yourself because eventually, someone is going to stand up with you."_

In Solidarity,
Nina Turner
President
Our Revolution

WASHINGTON, DC, 20002

name: Jay Inslee
subject: It's time to legalize marijuana.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
It's time to act on evidence, not on prejudice. | [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79cb3/2f5ea88d/73c1c587/2cbc9fcc/1446792155/VEsH/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/legalize-marijuana?utm_source=ema_2018-01-05_marijuana-legalization&source=ema_2018-01-05_marijuana-legalization&utm_campaign=marijuana-legalization&utm_medium=ema") Friend, I am telling Congress it is time to legalize marijuana. [**> > Join me in telling Congress to legalize marijuana now. <<**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79cb3/2f5ea88d/73c1c587/2cbc9fcc/1446792155/VEsE/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/legalize-marijuana?utm_source=ema_2018-01-05_marijuana-legalization&source=ema_2018-01-05_marijuana-legalization&utm_campaign=marijuana-legalization&utm_medium=ema") Attorney General Jeff Sessions has announced that he will be rescinding the Cole Memorandum. That decision impairs our ability to implement common-sense marijuana laws. And it penalizes medical marijuana patients and small businesses that are entirely compliant with state laws. **You know, I call foul. Republicans were the party that used to champion states' rights -- now, they're the party of hypocrites.** But here's what I promise you. Just like we said in 2012, when by a vote of the people we legalized marijuana, we will vigorously defend our state's laws against undue federal infringement. We are moving full speed ahead, and our program has seen incredible success -- our communities are safer, we have increased revenue for our state to use in funding education and health care, and our citizens have more freedom. Recently, I told Chuck Todd on MSNBC that marijuana legalization is working in Washington state. And it's time for the federal government to learn from us. Congress has to act now to legalize marijuana. There's clear evidence that this is working in every state where it has been well-regulated. Congress and this administration ought to act in accordance with the desires of the American people. We have shown in our state that when you regulate the market, you don't see an increase in youth usage, there's less criminal activity, and there is revenue benefit to the state budget. **We need leaders, including those in Washington, DC, to make decisions based on evidence, not on ideology and prejudice. The evidence says, it doesn't make sense to go backwards on marijuana.** [**Sign on to tell Congress: It's time to legalize marijuana. It's time to act on the evidence.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79cb3/2f5ea88d/73c1c587/2cbc9fcc/1446792155/VEsF/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/legalize-marijuana?utm_source=ema_2018-01-05_marijuana-legalization&source=ema_2018-01-05_marijuana-legalization&utm_campaign=marijuana-legalization&utm_medium=ema") Very truly yours, Jay Inslee **P.S. Can you get 5 friends to sign on, too? We need to show them the strength of our numbers.** | [SIGN ON »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79cb3/2f5ea88d/73c1c587/2cbc9fcc/1446792155/VEsC/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/legalize-marijuana?utm_source=ema_2018-01-05_marijuana-legalization&source=ema_2018-01-05_marijuana-legalization&utm_campaign=marijuana-legalization&utm_medium=ema")

name: Stephanie Schriock, EMILY's List
subject: The next 10 months
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6a6d/24c69fd9/13c287247/1892d6f4/2803124630/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFEM11N_digital_email_20180108_ALookAhead2018_inact&source=WQEFEM11N_digital_email_20180108_ALookAhead2018_inact")
---

Peter,

2018 is here, and it's time for action.

Last year, we began phase one of helping women run for office -- we mapped out the political landscape, put vulnerable Republicans "On Notice," figured out which races to target, and worked aggressively to recruit and train strong candidates to run in them.

We made lots of plans last year. Now it's time to start putting them into action.

**[Donate $3 now to help our candidates run strong campaigns that can power them to victory in November.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6a6d/24c69fd9/13c287247/1892d6f5/2803124630/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180108_email_ALookAhead2018?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+now+to+help+our+candidates+run+&utm_campaign=WQEFEM11N_digital_email_20180108_ALookAhead2018_inact&source=WQEFEM11N_digital_email_20180108_ALookAhead2018_inact")**

The next 10 months are going to be non-stop.

We have even more federal and gubernatorial campaigns to get off the ground. And our state and local team is working every day to recruit pro-choice Democratic women to run for 598 state legislative seats.

Primaries start in March and run all the way through September.

Polls will start coming out, ads will launch, and debates will be broadcast -- all before the final work of turning voters out.

It's no small undertaking for any campaign, and we are engaged in some of the most competitive races in the country -- with the opportunity to flip the House and Senate a real possibility.

Helping women win in 2018 depends on the work we do to invest in their campaigns today.

**Donate $3 now to help elect pro-choice Democratic women running for office across the country:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Help-Women-Win-2018](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6a6d/24c69fd9/13c287247/1892d6f6/2803124630/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180108_email_ALookAhead2018?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgHelpWomenWin2018&utm_campaign=WQEFEM11N_digital_email_20180108_ALookAhead2018_inact&source=WQEFEM11N_digital_email_20180108_ALookAhead2018_inact")**

Thanks,

Stephanie Schriock
President, EMILY's List
